name: Named Meditation Technique
summary: Named mind-hygiene techniques (pranayama, samatha, vipassana, etc.) the agent can invoke as discrete moves, distinct from a runtime meditation mode that pauses outward I/O. Each technique is a labelled internal action with its own protocol, callable explicitly.

raised_by.note: kitest carries two distinct modules: meditation_mode.py (runtime mode where I/O is paused) and meditation.py (library of named techniques). The catalog covers the mode but not the techniques.

rejected_reason: Subsumed as content rather than shape. meditation-mode already captures the structural pattern (bounded runtime mode where I/O pauses and inner tools take over). Pranayama, samatha, vipassana etc. are *which* internal moves run inside that mode — a library of named techniques is a deployment choice for the mode, not a separate agentic-design pattern with its own forces. No second independent source names 'named meditation technique' as a recurring agent design move.
